summary:
Perfect! The test now passes. I found and fixed the needle in the haystack:

The problem was an off-by-one error in the pagination logic in `app/app.py`. The faulty line was:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page - (1 if page > 1 else 0)
```

This was causing items to be duplicated across page boundaries. Page 1 would end at ID 10, but page 2 would start at ID 10 instead of ID 11.

I fixed it by correcting the offset calculation to:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page
```

This ensures proper pagination where page 1 contains items 1-10, page 2 contains items 11-20, and so on, with no gaps or overlaps.
